case_id: 5 chemicals: Chlorine, Ammonia, Phosgene, Methyl isocyanate, Sulfur dioxide

pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/2862) - authoritative NOAA hazard database

pi_items.verified_value: ground-level hazard (vapor density 2.49 > 1.0, heavier than air, will sink)
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals classification and physical properties
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4860) - EPA 1998 data

pi_items.verified_value: elevated disperser (vapor density 0.6 < 1.0, lighter than air, will rise)
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals classification: 'Lighter than air; will rise'
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4228) - EPA 1998 data; also confirmed by CDC/NIOSH Pocket Guide (3.42)

pi_items.verified_value: ground-level hazard (vapor density 3.4 > 1.0, heavier than air, will sink)
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals: 'Heavier than air; will sink'

pi_items.verified_value: approximately 2.0 (described as 'about twice as heavy as air')
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/1112) - EPA 1998 data

pi_items.verified_value: ground-level hazard (vapor density ~2.0 > 1.0, heavier than air, will sink)
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals: MIC is denser than air and will accumulate in low-lying areas
CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/1554) - EPA 1998 data; also verified by ChemicalBook (2.263)

pi_items.verified_value: ground-level hazard (vapor density 2.26 > 1.0, heavier than air, will sink)
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals: 'Heavier than air; will sink'

pi_items.verified_value: soluble in water (0.6-0.72 g/100mL at 20-25°C); confirmed rain-scrubbable
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals and hazmat scrubbing system documentation

pi_items.verified_value: poorly soluble (0.069 g.mole/liter·atm) but hydrolyzes to HCl and CO2 in water; atmospheric rain and moisture remove it
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals, EPA, ATSDR, and atmospheric chemistry research

pi_items.verified_value: reacts violently with water forming 1,3-dimethylurea and CO2 with heat evolution; emergency protocols explicitly state to avoid water contact and protect from rain
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/1112) and ATSDR Medical Management Guidelines

pi_items.verified_value: soluble in water, dissolves to form sulfurous acid; effectively removed by rain
pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals and water chemistry references
task.json metadata and export_result.sh verification script

privileged_info_summary: All five chemical vapor density values from task metadata are verified against CAMEO Chemicals (NOAA): Chlorine 2.49 (claimed 2.5), Ammonia 0.6 (claimed 0.6), Phosgene 3.4 (claimed 3.4), Methyl isocyanate ~2.0 (claimed 2.0), Sulfur dioxide 2.26 (claimed 2.3). All boiling points verified: Chlorine -30.3°F, Ammonia -28°F, Phosgene 47°F, Methyl isocyanate 102°F, Sulfur dioxide 14°F. All cloud behavior classifications (ground vs. elevated) are correct based on vapor density. Water solubility/rain scrubbing properties verified: Chlorine, Ammonia, and Sulfur dioxide are rain-scrubbable (soluble in water); Methyl isocyanate reacts violently with water and is not rain-scrubbable; Phosgene is poorly soluble but hydrolyzes in atmospheric moisture. Data source is real and public: CAMEO Chemicals database maintained by NOAA.
